response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and web engineer with a broad technology background, but he fundamentally does not meet the critical requirements for this role. The position mandates native-level Japanese language proficiency, which is entirely absent from his profile. His primary expertise lies in TypeScript, Node.js, and web platforms — not in C-based embedded firmware or Linux kernel development. He has no apparent background in the required security domains (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal) or in Layer 2/3 networking and LAN-switch architecture. While he mentions embedded systems and C/C++ work peripherally, these are brief historical entries and do not reflect the depth this role demands.
strengths: 14+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded contexts demonstrates adaptability and technical range
Some C/C++ background (National Instruments LabWindows/CVI, Sound Position Server thesis) shows foundational systems-level exposure
Strong Linux familiarity and experience with CI/CD, DevOps tooling, and open-source development reflects solid engineering practices
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — a hard, non-negotiable requirement explicitly listed in the job description
Virtually no experience with the required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, encryption, tamper detection) or L2/L3 networking/switch development
Career trajectory is firmly oriented toward web full-stack and cloud work (TypeScript, Node.js, React, AWS); embedded firmware and Linux kernel modification are absent from any meaningful role
